pr.title: Migrate all of HSD's id asm into the C file
pr.author: PsiLupan

summary: Moved the HSD ID module's standalone assembly from asm/id_asm.s into src/sysdolphin/id.c as inline asm, removed asm/id_asm.o from the build, exposed named HSD_ID APIs in id.h, and updated existing assembly/C call sites from anonymous func_8037xxxx labels to HSD_IDInsertToTable, HSD_IDRemoveByIDFromTable, and HSD_IDGetDataFromTable. The author also carried a NON_MATCHING C attempt for HSD_IDInsertToTable and noted an unresolved relocation error in the PR body.
what_changed: Deleted asm/id_asm.s, which contained the old standalone assembly for func_8037CDEC, func_8037CEE8, func_8037CF98, and the forget-memory routine.
Added the migrated ID routines to src/sysdolphin/id.c, including inline asm implementations for HSD_IDInsertToTable, HSD_IDRemoveByIDFromTable, HSD_IDGetDataFromTable, and _HSD_IDForgetMemory.
Kept a NON_MATCHING C implementation attempt for HSD_IDInsertToTable, showing the intended hash-table behavior while preserving inline asm for matching.
Removed $(BUILD_DIR)/asm/id_asm.o from TEXT_O_FILES in obj_files.mk so src/sysdolphin/id.o owns the ID code.
Added id.h declarations for HSD_IDInsertToTable, HSD_IDRemoveByIDFromTable, HSD_IDGetDataFromTable, and _HSD_IDForgetMemory.
Added #define hash(s) (s % 0x65) to id.h, matching the modulo-0x65 bucket logic visible in the migrated assembly.
Updated external call labels in existing asm/C files from func_8037CDEC to HSD_IDInsertToTable, func_8037CEE8 to HSD_IDRemoveByIDFromTable, and func_8037CF98 to HSD_IDGetDataFromTable.
Added #include "id.h" to src/sysdolphin/aobj.h and added the HSD_ObjFree prototype to src/sysdolphin/objalloc.h.
Renamed the comment for lbl_804C23C0 in id.c from alloc_data to hsd_iddata and added externs for lbl_804D5EE8 and lbl_804D5EF0 used by the migrated allocation failure path.
smart_moves: Centralized the ID module in src/sysdolphin/id.c instead of keeping a separate asm/id_asm.s object, reducing split ownership and making later C decompilation easier.
Updated all known call sites to semantic HSD_ID names while preserving branch targets and instruction comments, improving searchability without changing behavior.
Used NON_MATCHING for the partial C reconstruction instead of forcing a risky nonmatching rewrite into the matching build.
Kept force_active pragmas around routines that might otherwise be dropped, consistent with the existing HSD_IDSetup comment about matching unused functions.
Exposed prototypes in id.h so other modules can call the named HSD_ID APIs rather than relying on anonymous func_ labels.
decomp_lessons: When migrating a standalone asm file into a C translation unit, remember to remove the old asm object from obj_files.mk or duplicate symbols/order issues can occur.
Renaming call targets in remaining assembly files is useful decomp hygiene: it documents function purpose even before those caller files are decompiled.
A NON_MATCHING block can preserve high-level understanding of the algorithm while inline asm remains the authoritative matching implementation.
The ID table uses a default table when the table argument is NULL, so future rewrites should preserve that behavior.

The hash bucket calculation is semantically id % 0x65; the assembly implements this with a multiplication-by-magic sequence, so future matching attempts may need to account for compiler strength reduction.
Header work matters during asm-to-C migration: adding id.h includes/prototypes and objalloc.h's HSD_ObjFree declaration made cross-module references explicit.
naming_conventions: The reset/forget routine was exposed as _HSD_IDForgetMemory, using a leading underscore for the internal-style HSD helper.
The module uses HSD_ID* prefixes for public ID-table APIs and HSD_Obj* prefixes for allocator APIs.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Migrated raw asm into inline asm functions in src/sysdolphin/id.c with nofralloc to preserve exact prologue/control over generated code.
Preserved instruction address comments from asm/id_asm.s inside the inline asm bodies, making it easy to map migrated code back to the original disassembly.
Used #ifdef NON_MATCHING around the C version of HSD_IDInsertToTable and #else for the inline asm version.
Kept #pragma force_active around functions that need to remain linked for matching even if not directly referenced.
The migrated hash code corresponds to modulo 0x65, visible both as #define hash(s) (s % 0x65) and as the magic constant path using 0x446F8657 in assembly.
Call-site-only asm files were not decompiled here; they were minimally edited to replace anonymous branch labels with named HSD_ID symbols.
review_feedback: No issue comments, review comments, or reviews were present in the provided PR slice.
The PR body says the changes currently caused a relocation error for an undetermined reason; no later diagnostic discussion is included in the slice.
follow_up_queries: Find later PRs that remove the NON_MATCHING HSD_IDInsertToTable block or convert the inline asm ID routines to matching C.
Search for the relocation error mentioned in PR #56 to see whether it was diagnosed in a later branch or PR.
Check later changes to src/sysdolphin/id.h for refinements to HSD_IDTable, IDEntry, and the hash macro.
Look for subsequent renames of lbl_804C23C0, lbl_804C23EC, lbl_804D5EE8, and lbl_804D5EF0 to confirm allocator/default table/string symbol names.
